commentators.name: Savita Subramanian
commentators.key_quote: I like stocks, I just don't like the index right now.

commentators.key_quote: Friday's sharp equity selloff was a necessary and ultimately constructive development for the bull market's longevity.
key_views: Selloff was a healthy reset driven by positioning, not fundamentals.
Broader earnings and economic backdrop remain supportive.

commentators.name: Jurrien Timmer
commentators.key_quote: On one side is continued earnings strength... On the other side is inflation and interest rates.

commentators.name: Lance Roberts

commentators.key_quote: Do I think there's vulnerability in the market right now? Given a high bar of expectations and certainly some sentiment? Absolutely.

commentators.key_quote: We maintain a year-end target of 7,000 for the S&P 500, signalling modest gains.

Advises trimming gold and adding diversified U.S. equities.

commentators.name: David Rosenberg
commentators.key_quote: Markets are pricing continuity. Equities assume earnings hold up. Credit markets assume refinancing remains orderly.
key_views: Economy dependent on equity market performance; risk of recession if support fades.
Valuations are extreme (CAPE near 40), sentiment is 'all-in'.

commentators.name: Jurrien Timmer
commentators.key_quote: The market appears to be influenced by two competing forces. On one side is continued earnings strength... On the other side is inflation and interest rates.

AI advances contributing to productivity.
Higher inflation could lead to tighter policy and pressure valuations.

equity.synthesis: This week's commentary reveals a market at a crossroads, with significant disagreement on the near-term outlook. While some strategists like Mike Wilson see recent pullbacks as healthy resets within a continuing bull market, others, such as Savita Subramanian and Lance Roberts, are sounding alarms about high valuations and potential downturns, with Roberts even predicting a 'lost decade.' Tom Lee acknowledges potential near-term challenges but maintains a generally bullish stance for the current phase.
themes: Impact of AI on Market Leadership and Valuations

commentators.name: Michael Saylor
commentators.key_quote: It's a good time to add more dots.
key_views: Bitcoin's base layer should be treated as sacred infrastructure.
Recent weakness is a capital rotation into AI, not a Bitcoin impairment.
Volatility creates opportunity for disciplined expansion.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, sees current prices as attractive for accumulation.
commentators.price_target: Not specified, but implies a long-term upward trajectory.

commentators.name: Peter Schiff
commentators.key_quote: There is way too much complacency in Bitcoin for the market to be anywhere near a bottom.
key_views: Bitcoin could fall below $20,000 if it breaks $50,000.
Complacency suggests the market is far from a bottom.
Bitcoin's decline could signal broader trouble for risk assets.
commentators.btc_view: Extremely bearish, predicts a significant crash below $20,000.
commentators.eth_view: Not specified, but has previously been critical of crypto.

commentators.name: Anthony Pompliano
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin is maturing into a traditional finance asset, and institutional demand signals what mass adoption looks like.
key_views: Institutional adoption continues to mature and should support long-term crypto adoption.
Bitcoin is a savings technology and a hedge against fiat currency debasement.
Recurring declarations of Bitcoin being 'dead' are wrong.
commentators.btc_view: Very bullish, sees institutional adoption as a key driver.

commentators.name: PlanB
commentators.key_quote: IMO data is telling us that we have not seen bottom formation yet, and that there is a >50% probability that we go lower.
key_views: Market sentiment is split on whether the $60,000 level was the bottom.
Weakening derivatives activity and fragile market sentiment could push Bitcoin lower.
A deeper correction is possible before a strong bottom is formed.
commentators.btc_view: Cautious, sees a >50% probability of falling below $61,000.

commentators.name: Michael Novogratz
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin isn't dead. This is despite some experts like Peter Schiff saying so.
key_views: The traditional 4-year Bitcoin cycle may no longer be the right framework.
Bitcoin's resilience remains intact, but its structure is changing.
Crypto has lost investor attention as AI dominates market narratives.
commentators.btc_view: Neutral, believes the 4-year cycle is becoming less relevant.

commentators.name: Raoul Pal
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin has climbed roughly 318% since the 2022 liquidity-cycle low... So if capital was fleeing crypto for chips and AI names, it's doing a lousy job showing up in the data.
key_views: Bitcoin's long-term trajectory is stronger than many admit.
Global liquidity expansion rewards high-beta assets like Bitcoin.
The narrative of capital fleeing crypto for AI stocks is not supported by data.
commentators.btc_view: Bullish, sees Bitcoin as a macro asset tied to global monetary expansion.
commentators.price_target: Not specified, but implies significant long-term growth.

key_views: A major crypto surge is expected to begin after October 2026, driven by AI.
Bitcoin's core thesis remains intact despite market turbulence.
Capital will rotate back into crypto from tech stocks.
commentators.btc_view: Very bullish, anticipates a significant rally starting late 2026.

commentators.name: Cathie Wood
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin could reach $1.25 million in the next five years.
key_views: Institutional adoption is accelerating.
Bitcoin is an 'insurance policy' against currency debasement.

commentators.btc_view: Very bullish, with a $1.25 million price target within five years.

commentators.name: Reza Bundy
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin could drop 70% to the range of $26,000 to $30,000 within six months, before rebounding to $150,000 to $500,000 over the next few years.
key_views: Bitcoin has failed as an inflation hedge and is a volatile risk asset.
Short-term drop is possible due to macroeconomic factors.
Long-term outlook is bullish, depending on global economic conditions.

commentators.name: Nouriel Roubini
commentators.key_quote: Bitcoin has failed as an inflation hedge and is currently just a highly volatile risk asset, fluctuating in sync with tech stocks.
key_views: Bitcoin is a speculative asset, not an inflation hedge.
It fluctuates in sync with tech stocks.
Forecasts a significant short-term drop.
commentators.btc_view: Very bearish, sees it as a volatile risk asset.
commentators.price_target: Implied to be significantly lower, aligned with Reza Bundy's $26k-$30k range.

commentators.name: Nic Carter
commentators.key_quote: I don't think it's game over for Zcash. It's basically part of the deal.
key_views: Privacy coins face inherent trade-offs between confidentiality and transparency.
Vulnerabilities in privacy coins are part of the deal.
The market reaction to Zcash vulnerability was significant.

crypto.synthesis: This past week saw a divergence of opinions on Bitcoin's trajectory. While some, like Michael Saylor and Anthony Pompliano, remain strongly bullish, viewing current dips as accumulation opportunities and signs of maturing institutional adoption, others, notably Peter Schiff and Nouriel Roubini, maintain a deeply bearish outlook, predicting significant price drops and questioning Bitcoin's fundamental value. A more cautious stance was taken by PlanB, who sees a greater than 50% chance of further downside before a bottom is formed.
themes: Short-term Bearish vs. Long-term Bullish Outlooks
Impact of AI on Market Narratives
Bitcoin's Role as a Macro Asset
